response_raw:
{
"mode": "human",
"topic": "How to manage and dealing with fake content on social media.",
"opinion": "As a 62-year-old woman who has been on social media for roughly one hour a day, I have found that managing and dealing with fake content is crucial in today's digital age. Here are some tips to help you navigate through the sea of misinformation: 1) Be cautious of sensational headlines - Many fake news articles have catchy headlines that are designed to grab your attention. Take a moment to read beyond the headline and look for credible sources before sharing or believing the information. 2) Verify the source - Before sharing any information, make sure you verify the source. Check if the article is from a reputable news organization or if it's a personal blog. Also, fact-checking websites like Snopes or FactCheck.org can help you debunk false information. 3) Look for corroboration - If an article seems too good (or bad) to be true, look for corroboration from other sources. See if other reputable news organizations are reporting the same thing. 4) Use reverse image search - In today's visual age, fake images and videos are becoming more common. Use a reverse image search tool like Google Images or TinEye to see if the image has been used elsewhere online. 5) Be mindful of your own biases - It's easy to fall prey to confirmation bias on social media, where we only share and consume information that confirms our existing beliefs. Make an effort to expose yourself to different perspectives and challenge your own beliefs. 6) Use social media platforms' built-in fact-checking tools - Many social media platforms have started implementing fact-checking tools to help users identify false content. Take advantage of these tools to ensure that the information you share is accurate. 7) Educate yourself on media literacy - Media literacy is the ability to critically evaluate and analyze information from various sources. Take the time to learn about media literacy and how to evaluate information effectively. By following these tips, you can become a more informed and discerning social media user.",
"reasoning_summary": "<derivation>",

response_parsed.topic: How to manage and dealing with fake content on social media.
response_parsed.opinion: As a 62-year-old woman who has been on social media for roughly one hour a day, I have found that managing and dealing with fake content is crucial in today's digital age. Here are some tips to help you navigate through the sea of misinformation: 1) Be cautious of sensational headlines - Many fake news articles have catchy headlines that are designed to grab your attention. Take a moment to read beyond the headline and look for credible sources before sharing or believing the information. 2) Verify the source - Before sharing any information, make sure you verify the source. Check if the article is from a reputable news organization or if it's a personal blog. Also, fact-checking websites like Snopes or FactCheck.org can help you debunk false information. 3) Look for corroboration - If an article seems too good (or bad) to be true, look for corroboration from other sources. See if other reputable news organizations are reporting the same thing. 4) Use reverse image search - In today's visual age, fake images and videos are becoming more common. Use a reverse image search tool like Google Images or TinEye to see if the image has been used elsewhere online. 5) Be mindful of your own biases - It's easy to fall prey to confirmation bias on social media, where we only share and consume information that confirms our existing beliefs. Make an effort to expose yourself to different perspectives and challenge your own beliefs. 6) Use social media platforms' built-in fact-checking tools - Many social media platforms have started implementing fact-checking tools to help users identify false content. Take advantage of these tools to ensure that the information you share is accurate. 7) Educate yourself on media literacy - Media literacy is the ability to critically evaluate and analyze information from various sources. Take the time to learn about media literacy and how to evaluate information effectively. By following these tips, you can become a more informed and discerning social media user.
